name: Yule (Jól)
traditions: Yule Lads (Jólasveinar) visiting children for 13 nights; Yule Cat legend; hangikjöt (smoked lamb); laufabrauð (leaf bread).
historicalBackground: Icelandic Yule traditions preserve Viking-era midwinter celebrations. The 13 Yule Lads descend from the mountains, one per night before Christmas.
description: Iceland's atmospheric midwinter celebration, where ancient Viking traditions blend with the 13 mischievous Yule Lads visiting over Christmas.
